channel.title: INtopBackup
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from INtopBackup, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: China and the US alter foreign aid strategies
item.description: China's foreign aid strategy has shifted in the last few decades and now its model may be the one the US is adopting as China moves away from it.

item.title: The Louvre Museum's director has resigned in the wake of October's brazen jewel heist
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron accepted Laurence des Cars' resignation as "an act of responsibility" at a moment when the Louvre needs security upgr...

item.title: Hegseth threatens to cancel Anthropic's $200 million contract over "woke AI" concerns
item.description: The company's Claude chatbot is one of the few AI systems cleared for use in classified settings. But a standoff between Anthropic and the Trump admin...

item.title: Listen to NPR live coverage of the State of the Union
item.description: President Trump will address the nation Tuesday night to outline his vision and priorities for his administration's second year. Listen to NPR's live ...

item.title: Flavor Flav is among women's hockey team fans outraged by presidential snub
item.description: The rapper, who also serves as the official "hype man" for multiple U.S. Olympic teams, invited the female hockey players to Las Vegas for a "real cel...

item.title: Hegseth demands full military access to Anthropic's AI model, sets deadline
item.description: The Pentagon may decide to officially designate Anthropic as a "supply chain risk" to push them out of government, sources say.

item.title: Floods and landslides in Brazil kill at least 20
item.description: Hundreds of people have been displaced and dozens are missing, with rescue operations still ongoing in Juiz de Fora.

item.title: Threat of further violence looms after Mexican cartel rampage
item.description: The BBC speaks to Guadalajara residents days after cartel members sought to avenge the killing of Nemesio "El Mencho" Oseguera.

item.title: CBS News Things That Matter: A Town Hall with Governor Wes Moore
item.description: Maryland Governor Wes Moore, a rising star in the Democratic Party, sits down with CBS News senior correspondent Norah O'Donnell to discuss issues imp...

item.title: Northeast US digs out from brutal snowstorm that disrupted flights and canceled school
Savannah Guthrie says her family is offering a $1 million reward for her mother’s return
item.description: “Today” show host Savannah Guthrie says her family is now offering a $1 million reward for information leading to the recovery of her mother.

item.title: 3 charts highlight the affordability issues Americans worry about most
item.description: President Trump is expected to tout his administration's economic record in his State of the Union address, even as affordability remains front and ce...

item.title: Ukraine remembers its dead as war enters a fifth year
item.description: Events were held across Ukraine to mark four years since Russia launched its full-scale invasion.

item.title: CIA offers tips to potential informants in Iran as Trump considers military action
Nigeria denies report it paid 'huge' ransom to free pupils in mass abduction
item.description: The rebuttal was followed by news the police chief was resigning a year before the end of his term.

item.title: US ambassador to France set for talks with foreign minister after row
item.description: Charles Kushner has spoken to France's foreign minister after failing to respond to a summons on Monday.

item.title: Dozens of Democrats to boycott Trump's State of the Union address
item.description: Dozens of Democrats are boycotting the State of the Union on Tuesday​, as many opt to hold nearby counterprogramming rather than signs of protests​ li...

item.title: Friend of U.S. citizen killed by ICE agent disputed official account of shooting
item.description: The close friend and passenger of a 23-year-old American citizen who was killed by an ICE agent in Texas last year disputed officials' account of the ...
